lens: ssl-representation: make the masked-SSL that already won smarter (better surrogate input, curriculum masking, auxiliary structure heads, difficulty-aware conditioning/dropout) — all as concrete edits to train.py / pdmx_dataset.py, no architecture change, attacking the Scriabin miss-rate, Mozart over-production, val!=MUSTER, and unmeasured-pieces residuals.

proposals.title: Curriculum masking of the unpaired surrogate: reveal quantized onset early, anneal to pitch-only
proposals.mechanism: Today UnpairedScoreDataset._load_pair masks the unpaired encoder input to a constant (onset=0, duration=0, velocity=64), keeping only score pitch — the maximally information-poor surrogate (the paper's pitch-only setting). That makes the model learn 'recover everything from pitch alone', which is fine for easy homophonic content but gives no signal for placing dense fast notes, exactly where our miss-rate concentrates (Scriabin). Add a curriculum: at the START of training, feed the unpaired branch a WEAK but real structural hint — the score's own QUANTIZED onset (and optionally duration) bucketed onto the existing 1/24 grid — then PROGRESSIVELY mask it back to the pitch-only constant as global_step grows. Concretely: in UnpairedScoreDataset add a settable attribute reveal_prob (0..1); with prob reveal_prob keep the real (quantized) onset/duration in input_stream instead of zeroing them; otherwise mask as now. Drive reveal_prob from a callback that reads trainer.global_step (linear anneal 1.0 -> 0.0 over the first ~60% of steps). This is classic easy-to-hard curriculum / scheduled-sampling for masked reconstruction: start with a solvable reconstruction (timing visible) so the encoder learns a strong note-placement representation, then remove the crutch so the final model still runs pitch-only at inference (surrogate stays pitch-only at test, unchanged). Because at inference the real performance MIDI provides true onsets anyway, revealing quantized onsets during unpaired training does NOT create a train/test surrogate mismatch on the PAIRED inference path.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin miss-rate on dense/fast passages (the dominant residual) — gives the representation an early, learnable signal for placing many close-spaced notes; secondarily reduces the pitch-only information starvation that forces guessing.
proposals.how_to_run: Edit MIDI2ScoreTransformer/midi2scoretransformer/pdmx_dataset.py: in UnpairedScoreDataset add self.reveal_prob=0.0 and, in _load_pair, draw r=random.random(); if r<self.reveal_prob keep input_stream['onset']/['duration'] (the cached real score values) else zero them (current behavior); always still mask velocity. In train.py add a tiny pl.Callback (on_train_batch_start) that sets every UnpairedScoreDataset.reveal_prob = max(0.0, 1.0 - global_step/(0.6*total_steps)) (reach the datasets via train_loader.dataset's ConcatDataset.datasets[1].ds). Run: python train.py fit --stage ssl_curric_reveal --dataset-type ssl --manifest data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv --init-ckpt <released-or-none> --lr 3e-4 --max-epochs 16 --real-fraction 0.5 --warmup-steps 800 --out-dir checkpoints/ssl_curric_reveal. Then scripts/robust_eval.sh on the 14 ASAP test pieces, A/B vs ssl_classical_clean.
proposals.cost: ~1 training run, same length as ssl_classical_clean (~12-16 epochs). Roughly 6-10 A100-hours.
proposals.grounding: The 1/24 step grid already encodes triplet/tuplet-divisible positions (config.FEATURES onset/offset/downbeat step_size=1/24), so a quantized-onset hint is exactly the structure dense tuplets need. Curriculum/scheduled-sampling for masked reconstruction is well established (BERT-style mask-rate schedules; curriculum learning, Bengio 2009). The change is local and the paper itself shows the surrogate's information content matters (+1.84 E_avg pitch-vs-zeroed, their Table 6) — so adding then removing more information is a principled extension of the lever that already won.
proposals.expected_outcome: Scriabin MeanER drops from 12.3 toward the released 10.37 with Mozart held at ~5.76 (no over-production, since the curriculum ends at the same pitch-only surrogate). We'd know from per-piece robust_eval: success = Scriabin <=11.5 AND Mozart <=6.0 AND tuplet count on Mozart stays ~36 (not 166). Risk flagged separately.
proposals.risk: If the anneal ends too late, the model may lean on the revealed onset and regress on the true pitch-only inference path; mitigate by ensuring reveal_prob hits 0 well before training ends and validating on real ASAP (already pitch-driven).

proposals.title: Auxiliary tuplet/beat-phase prediction head trained ONLY from the existing offset stream (no new labels, no architecture change at inference)
proposals.mechanism: Our error is 'miss notes on dense passages', and both models emit ~0 tuplets — the representation never has to commit to sub-beat metric placement. Add a self-supervised AUXILIARY LOSS that forces the shared decoder representation to predict each note's beat-phase / tuplet-class, derived for FREE from the score's own offset stream (within-measure offset, already computed in bucket_mxl on the 1/24 grid). Concretely: define a derived target tuplet_class = is_the_offset_a_non-dyadic-subdivision (e.g. offset*24 mod 24 not in {0,12,6,18,...} dyadic set -> triplet/quintuplet bucket). This is computable in _compute_loss from output_streams['offset'] (argmax index) with zero new data. Reuse the EXISTING offset/downbeat output heads' logits: add an auxiliary CE term that sharpens prediction of the non-dyadic offset buckets specifically (upweight the loss on tuplet-grid offset classes via a per-class weight vector in the offset CE). No new module, no new head at inference — it is purely a reweighting + an auxiliary consistency term on heads the model already has. This makes the SSL objective multi-task: reconstruct the score AND correctly localize tuplet-grid positions, improving the representation's metric resolution on dense passages.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin tuplet tail + miss-rate: by forcing the representation to resolve sub-beat (tuplet-grid) offsets rather than collapsing them to the nearest dyadic bucket, the model is less likely to drop/merge the dense fast notes that share close offsets. Also directly attacks the '~0 of 99 tuplets emitted' phenomenon.
proposals.how_to_run: Edit train.py _compute_loss: build a class-weight vector w_offset over the 145 offset buckets that upweights non-dyadic (tuplet-grid) buckets by a factor alpha (start alpha=3). Pass weight=w_offset into F.cross_entropy for the offset stream (and similarly downbeat). Gate it behind a --tuplet-aux flag with --tuplet-aux-alpha. Run two configs (alpha=2, alpha=4) from the ssl_classical_clean corpus: python train.py fit --stage ssl_tupletaux --dataset-type ssl --manifest data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv --lr 3e-4 --max-epochs 14 --real-fraction 0.5 --tuplet-aux --tuplet-aux-alpha 3 --out-dir checkpoints/ssl_tupletaux. Eval with robust_eval.sh and count emitted tuplets per piece (the eyeball_check / detokenize path already exposes tuplet counts).
proposals.cost: 1-2 training runs (~6-10 A100-hours each). Code change is ~15 lines.
proposals.grounding: The offset stream is already the within-measure metric position on a 1/24 (triplet-divisible) grid (tokenizer.py PARAMS), so tuplet membership is recoverable with no new labels — this is genuinely self-supervised. Class-balanced / focal reweighting is the standard fix for a rare-token tail (tuplet buckets are rare), and multi-task auxiliary structure prediction is a well-grounded representation booster in music SSL. It is strictly cheaper and safer than the tested-negative 'more dense data' because it does NOT change the data distribution (so it should NOT cause Mozart over-production the way curated dense data did).
proposals.expected_outcome: Model begins emitting a non-zero (but calibrated) number of tuplets on Scriabin and lowers its MeanER toward 10.37, WITHOUT Mozart over-production (data distribution unchanged). Success metric: Scriabin tuplet emission goes from ~0 to >20 of 99 and MeanER improves >=0.5, while Mozart tuplet count stays ~36 and Mozart MeanER within 0.2 of 5.76.
proposals.risk: Over-aggressive alpha could induce over-production on simple pieces (the same failure mode as dense data); sweep alpha and pick the largest value that leaves Mozart tuplet count <=~50.

proposals.title: Difficulty-aware conditioning token + per-sample decoder dropout (turn the binary c_i into a graded density signal)
proposals.mechanism: The val!=MUSTER and over-production findings say the model's DECODING CALIBRATION is mismatched to piece difficulty: ssl_bigc had best val but over-produced on simple Mozart. Right now the conditioning token 'unconditional' is binary (1 unpaired / 0 paired) and decoder prior-token dropout is a single per-branch scalar (0.75/0.50). Replace the binary flag with a GRADED scalar = the score chunk's note-density / tuplet-fraction (cheap to compute from the cached stream: notes-per-beat and fraction of non-dyadic offsets), normalized to [0,1]. The 'unconditional' Linear already accepts a float (it's nn.Linear(1, d)), so feeding a graded value is a no-op architecturally. ALSO make the decoder prior-token dropout per-sample a function of that density: HARDER (denser) chunks get LOWER dropout (more visible prior tokens -> easier reconstruction of dense passages), simpler chunks get higher dropout. At inference, set the conditioning token to a fixed neutral value (as the paper sets c=0 for inference) OR expose it as an --difficulty knob in infer_with_ckpt.py so we can A/B whether telling the model 'this piece is dense' at test time changes Scriabin vs Mozart behavior.
proposals.attacks: Mozart over-production AND Scriabin miss-rate simultaneously — it gives the model an explicit density coordinate so it stops applying a one-size-fits-all notation prior (the root cause of over-producing tuplets on simple pieces while under-producing on dense ones). Also a direct, cheap probe of val!=MUSTER (calibration).
proposals.how_to_run: Edit _WithConditioning to accept a per-sample value instead of a constant flag: compute density = clamp(notes_per_beat/ref, 0,1) and tuplet_frac from inp streams, set inp['unconditional']=full((T,1), value). In train.py _maybe_drop_decoder, when ssl_mode, set per-row rate = base - k*density (e.g. 0.75 -> 0.55 for densest). Add --difficulty-cond flag. Run: python train.py fit --stage ssl_diffcond --dataset-type ssl --manifest data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv --lr 3e-4 --max-epochs 14 --real-fraction 0.5 --difficulty-cond. Then in scripts/infer_with_ckpt.py add --difficulty (default neutral) and A/B Scriabin at difficulty=high vs Mozart at difficulty=low on the box. Eval via robust_eval.sh.
proposals.cost: 1 training run (~6-10 A100-hours) + a few CPU-cheap inference sweeps over the --difficulty knob (no extra training).
proposals.grounding: The conditioning Linear is bias-free nn.Linear(1,d) (embedding.py:19) so a graded scalar is exactly representable and reduces to the current binary at the extremes — zero architecture risk and warm-start compatible. Density/tuplet-fraction are trivially computable from cached streams. Conditioning generation on a difficulty/style scalar (classifier-free-guidance-style conditioning, FiLM-like control) is well grounded; here it's a near-free extension of the lever that already won. It directly targets the observed calibration symptom (best val != best MUSTER, over-production).
proposals.expected_outcome: Mozart tuplet over-production is suppressed (count near 36) and we gain a TEST-TIME knob: setting --difficulty high should let Scriabin emit more structure without retraining. Success: at a single fixed neutral conditioning we match ssl_classical_clean on Mozart/Ravel, and the --difficulty sweep produces a monotone Scriabin response (more emitted notes/tuplets as difficulty rises) — evidence the representation now encodes a usable density axis.
proposals.risk: The density signal could be too weak (one scalar) to move the dense tail; if so it still gives the calibration probe and costs only one run. Worst case it is neutral like the tested beat-conditioning fine-tune.

proposals.title: Checkpoint selection by a MUSTER-proxy (over-production calibration metric) instead of val/total — close the val!=MUSTER gap with zero new training
proposals.mechanism: We KNOW val/total is the wrong selector (ssl_bigc best val 0.4996 but worst MUSTER via over-production; ssl_classical_clean epoch 13 is the validated best). The cheapest high-value lever is not a new objective but a better SELECTION criterion that correlates with MUSTER. During validation, in addition to CE, compute a cheap OVER-PRODUCTION / CALIBRATION proxy on the held-out real ASAP val set: (a) emitted-note-count ratio = predicted real-positions (pad>thresh) / target real-positions, and (b) tuplet-bucket emission ratio = predicted non-dyadic-offset notes / target. A model that over-produces has ratio >> 1. Log a composite score = val_CE + lambda*|log(note_ratio)| and select the checkpoint that minimizes IT, and ALSO save top-k by this proxy. This requires NO new training — just an added validation_step computation and re-scanning existing epoch checkpoints. It directly operationalizes the memory note 'the right ckpt may be an EARLIER epoch with lower over-production'.
proposals.attacks: val!=MUSTER and Mozart over-production directly: it changes WHICH epoch we ship so we stop selecting over-producing checkpoints, and it gives a fast surrogate so we don't burn full MUSTER eval on every epoch.
proposals.how_to_run: Edit train.py validation_step: from pred['pad'] (sigmoid>0.5) and target['pad'] compute note_ratio per batch; from pred['offset'].argmax identify non-dyadic buckets to compute tuplet_ratio; self.log('val/note_ratio') and 'val/over_production'=abs(log(note_ratio)). Add a second ModelCheckpoint(monitor='val/over_production'+'val/total' composite via a tiny LambdaCallback that writes a combined metric). FIRST, with NO retraining, run the existing per-epoch checkpoints of ssl_classical_clean / ssl_bigc through this proxy offline (load each ckpt, run one val pass) and correlate proxy vs the known MUSTER numbers to confirm the proxy ranks them correctly; then adopt it. Use scripts/robust_eval.sh only on the 2-3 proxy-selected epochs to confirm.
proposals.cost: Essentially FREE — no training. A handful of CPU/GPU validation passes over existing checkpoints (minutes each).
proposals.grounding: We have direct evidence the proxy will work: ssl_bigc (over-producing, note_ratio high) had the best val but worst MUSTER — so a note-count-ratio penalty would have correctly demoted it. The pad/offset heads needed for the proxy already exist and are exactly the streams MUSTER's miss/extra components score. This is the lowest-cost, highest-confidence item: it can only improve selection and is validated against numbers we already have.
proposals.expected_outcome: We confirm (or refute) that an EARLIER, lower-over-production epoch beats the val-selected one on MUSTER, and we get a cheap selector for all future runs. Success: the proxy's ranking of {ssl_bigc, ssl_classical_clean, earlier epochs} matches their known MUSTER order; if it surfaces a better epoch of an EXISTING run, that's an immediate free MUSTER gain.
proposals.risk: The proxy might correlate imperfectly; mitigate by validating it against the MUSTER numbers we already have before trusting it. No training cost, so downside is near zero.

proposals.title: Denoising-SSL variant: corrupt the surrogate with realistic performance noise so the encoder learns to be robust to dense fast passages (and measure the full 14-piece standing)
proposals.mechanism: The unpaired surrogate is a CLEAN pitch-only input; the real inference input is a NOISY human performance (jittered onsets, dropped/extra notes, pedal smear) — exactly the regime where we drop ~14% of notes. Make the SSL objective a DENOISING autoencoder over the unpaired branch: instead of masking timing to a constant, inject realistic performance-like corruption into the surrogate and ask the decoder to reconstruct the CLEAN score. Concretely, on the unpaired branch apply (i) onset jitter on a quantized grid, (ii) random local note DROPOUT (delete a few pitches) and INSERTION (duplicate/neighbor pitches), and (iii) chord-roll/asynchrony — all cheap stream ops in _load_pair. This teaches the encoder representation to recover the intended dense passage from a corrupted observation, which is the literal failure on Scriabin. Critically, scale corruption strength UP with chunk density (denser chunks get more dropout-style corruption) so the model practices precisely the dense-passage recovery it currently fails. Pair this run with the FIRST full 14-piece MUSTER eval (we have only scored 4-6 pieces, so we don't know our real standing) to convert 'unmeasured pieces' into signal.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin miss-rate primarily (denoising = note-recovery practice under dropout/insertion) AND the unmeasured-pieces gap (the run is the occasion to score all 14 test pieces vs released).
proposals.how_to_run: Edit UnpairedScoreDataset._load_pair to add a corrupt_strength attribute; implement note-level dropout/insertion and onset jitter on the surrogate streams (reuse the onset_jitter logic already in pdmx_dataset.py; add a pitch-drop/insert helper). Keep output_stream = clean score (denoising target). Density-scale corrupt_strength from cached notes_per_beat. Run: python train.py fit --stage ssl_denoise --dataset-type ssl --manifest data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv --lr 3e-4 --max-epochs 14 --real-fraction 0.5 --out-dir checkpoints/ssl_denoise. Then run scripts/robust_eval.sh over ALL 14 ASAP test pieces for BOTH ssl_denoise and the released ckpt (apples-to-apples) to get our first complete standing.
proposals.cost: 1 training run (~6-10 A100-hours) + one full 14-piece eval pass (the eval itself is CPU/music21-bound, a few hours but no GPU training).
proposals.grounding: Denoising autoencoding (BART/MASS-style corrupt-then-reconstruct, and denoising-SSL generally) is a proven way to learn robust representations; here the corruption is matched to the actual test distribution (noisy human performance) rather than the clean pitch-only surrogate, narrowing the train/inference surrogate gap the paper itself identifies as the key variable. It does NOT add dense data (so should avoid the over-production failure) — it adds dense-recovery PRACTICE. Running the full 14-piece eval is independently valuable: the memory explicitly notes we've only scored 4-6 pieces.
proposals.expected_outcome: Lower miss-rate on dense pieces -> Scriabin toward 10.37 and ideally improvements on the currently-unmeasured pieces; plus the first complete 14-piece scorecard vs released (which alone may reveal we are at/above parity on more pieces than we think, or expose a second residual). Success: Scriabin MeanER improves >=0.5 with Mozart held, and we obtain all 14 per-piece numbers for both models.
proposals.risk: If corruption is too strong it could blur clean simple pieces; density-scaling and a strength sweep (0.5x, 1x) mitigate. Even if training is neutral, the full 14-piece eval is a guaranteed informational win.

lens: data-ceiling: break the 822-real-aligned-pair ceiling without hand-aligning, via self-training/pseudo-labels and renderer-minted exactly-aligned pairs, with strict confidence filtering and MUSTER-based selection

proposals.title: Noisy-student self-training: pseudo-label MAESTRO performances with ssl_classical_clean, keep only high-confidence pairs, fold into the REAL branch
proposals.mechanism: Run ssl_classical_clean (the validated best ckpt) in inference over the ~200hr unaligned MAESTRO performance-MIDIs to produce (performance-MIDI, predicted-score) pairs. The model's generate() already exposes per-token, per-stream softmax probabilities (model.py:132) and a sigmoid pad/note-presence probability (model.py:135) — so we get a free, calibrated confidence signal with NO extra code: log the mean per-note pad-prob and mean pitch-prob during generation. Keep a pseudo-pair ONLY if (a) note-count is plausible vs the input MIDI note-count (|pred_notes - input_notes|/input_notes < 0.15, i.e. miss-rate guardrail — directly the Scriabin failure mode), (b) mean note-presence prob > 0.9 AND mean pitch-prob > 0.8, and (c) it round-trips: re-tokenize the predicted MusicXML and confirm it parses in music21 (drop ~the 10-15% that don't). Then ADD the surviving pairs to the REAL (flag=0, paired) branch of make_ssl_loaders — they enter as genuine aligned pairs, NOT the unpaired surrogate. Cap them so real-ASAP still dominates the paired sub-mix (e.g. 822 ASAP : <=3000 pseudo, and downweight pseudo 0.3x in the WeightedRandomSampler) to bound error amplification. Retrain from scratch with the identical exposure-matched recipe.
proposals.attacks: 822 real-aligned-pair ceiling (the binding constraint) + Scriabin miss-rate (the note-count guardrail explicitly selects against the drop-notes failure)
proposals.how_to_run: 1) Pseudo-label: for f in MAESTRO MIDIs: scripts/infer_with_ckpt.py f --ckpt checkpoints/ssl_classical_clean/...ep13.ckpt --out pseudo/f.musicxml --pad-threshold 0.5 --top-k 1 --temperature 1.0, and add a ~10-line patch to dump mean pad-prob + mean pitch-prob per piece to a sidecar JSON (probs are already computed at model.py:132/135; just reduce-mean and write). 2) Filter with a new scripts/filter_pseudo.py implementing the 3 gates above (note-count ratio from pretty_midi vs predicted, prob thresholds from sidecar, music21 parse check). 3) Build data/pairs_pseudo/ + cache via the existing build_asap_cache.py / make_pairs.py path so they tokenize byte-identically. 4) Extend make_ssl_loaders to concat real_train with a pseudo_train branch (flag=0.0, downweight 0.3x). 5) train.py --stage st1 --dataset-type ssl --manifest data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv (+pseudo via the new branch) --warmup ... matching the exposure-matched recipe. 6) Eval with robust_eval.sh on all 14 pieces. Quarantine note: MAESTRO is CC-BY-NC-SA -> NON-COMMERCIAL; tag these pairs so they never enter a shippable ckpt (lab/research use only, consistent with ACCURACY_ROADMAP quarantine).
proposals.cost: ~6-10 GPU-hours pseudo-labelling (200hr / ~hundreds of pieces, batched on the A100) + 1 training run ~8-12 GPU-hours = ~15-20 GPU-hours total
proposals.grounding: Noisy-student self-training (Xie et al. 2020) and pseudo-labelling are established for low-resource sequence tasks; in AMT specifically, semi-supervised/self-training on unlabeled audio (e.g. Cheuk et al. ReconVAT 2021; Maman & Bermano 'Unaligned Supervision' 2022) improved transcription beyond the labeled ceiling. Here the per-stream softmax/sigmoid confidence is ALREADY computed in generate(), so filtering is nearly free. The note-count gate is grounded in OUR diagnosis (error = miss-rate on dense passages, not tuplet tokens), so it preferentially harvests the EASY-medium pieces the model already handles and uses them to broaden the paired ceiling — the exact thing 822 pairs can't provide. Risk of error amplification is bounded by the 3 gates + 0.3x downweight + real-ASAP-dominant sub-mix.
proposals.expected_outcome: If it works: broad-accuracy improvement on the common-case pieces (Mozart/Ravel hold parity or improve; the 8 unmeasured pieces improve) with val NOT necessarily improving (since pseudo-pairs are noisier than ASAP). We'd know by all-14-piece MUSTER mean dropping vs ssl_classical_clean, with NO Mozart over-production regression (tuplet-count check stays ~36). Failure signal = pseudo-pairs drag mean up despite gates -> tighten thresholds or abandon. The note-count gate makes a Scriabin miss-rate gain plausible but not guaranteed.

proposals.title: Renderer-minted EXACTLY-aligned pairs at a SMALL fraction in the PAIRED branch (alignment signal, domain gap subordinated)
proposals.mechanism: The tested-negative synthetic-render levers all FAILED because synthetic pairs DOMINATED the mix (real_frac too low) and the deadpan/rubato domain gap took over (ar_rubato diverged, ar_full4/5 regressed). The untested variant: use OUR renderer (scripts/expressive_render.py) to mint a SMALL number of perfectly-aligned (synthetic-performance, score) pairs from the genre-matched CLASSICAL corpus already on the box (data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv scores), and inject them into the PAIRED (flag=0) branch at <=10% of paired samples — just enough to give the decoder extra EXACT onset/duration alignment supervision on classical notation, while real ASAP (822) still anchors >=90% of the paired sub-mix and the unpaired-SSL branch is unchanged at 50%. The hypothesis from our own data: the prior failures injected synthetic at 26-100% of the training signal; at <=5% of TOTAL samples the alignment signal helps without the domain gap dominating.
proposals.attacks: 822 ceiling (adds exactly-aligned classical pairs the ASAP set lacks) + Scriabin timing residual (exact alignment supervision targets the timing component of the per-stream error)
proposals.how_to_run: 1) Render: scripts/expressive_render.py over ~2000 scores sampled from data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv -> data/pairs_render_aligned/ (.mid + .musicxml + chunks). 2) Tokenize via build_asap_cache.py path. 3) Add a third branch to make_ssl_loaders (flag=0.0) weighted so render pairs are <=10% of the paired half (i.e. <=5% of all samples), real ASAP fills the rest. 4) train.py --dataset-type ssl with the exposure-matched recipe from run_ssl_classical_clean.sh. 5) robust_eval.sh on all 14 pieces; specifically check Mozart tuplet count stays ~36 (over-production guard) and Scriabin timing-ER component.
proposals.cost: ~3-5 GPU-hours rendering (CPU-bound, use the 252-core box) + 1 training run ~8-12 GPU-hours = ~12-17 GPU-hours
proposals.grounding: Grounded in the SPECIFIC failure mode of our own negative results: every failed synthetic run had synthetic at 26-100% of signal (ar_full5 kern×30=26%, ar_rubato 7.9% but warm-started a converged ckpt which is itself a known-dead vehicle). None tested synthetic at <=5% of TOTAL from-scratch alongside the SSL branch. Self-training/data-augmentation literature (and our SSL win itself) shows small auxiliary aligned signal can help when it doesn't dominate. This is the one synthetic-render configuration NOT in the tested-negative set.
proposals.expected_outcome: Plausibly a small timing-ER and miss-rate improvement on mid-density pieces with Mozart over-production NOT triggered (the small fraction is the whole point). We'd know by all-14 MUSTER mean vs ssl_classical_clean AND the Mozart tuplet count staying ~36. Marked low-confidence because synthetic-render is heavily tested-negative; the only novelty is the fraction, so the expected value is a modest hedge, not a likely big win. Kill fast if Mozart over-produces or mean regresses.

proposals.title: MUSTER-selected checkpointing + over-production calibration sweep (turn val!=MUSTER into a free gain, zero training)
proposals.mechanism: Two known facts combine into a no-train win: (1) val!=MUSTER — ssl_bigc had the BEST val (0.4996) but WORSE MUSTER because it over-produced; ssl_classical_clean ep13 was picked by val. The right ckpt is likely an EARLIER epoch with lower over-production. (2) over-production is a DECODING/calibration symptom. So: (a) sweep ALL saved epochs of ssl_classical_clean (save_top_k=2 + save_last means several are on disk; if only 2 remain, the from-scratch run is cheap to re-run logging every epoch) and select by all-14-piece MUSTER mean, NOT val/total; (b) for the chosen ckpt, sweep the inference knobs already exposed — --pad-threshold (raising it suppresses spurious notes = anti-over-production; lowering it recovers dropped notes = anti-miss-rate on Scriabin), --top-k, --temperature — per-piece, to find whether a single global setting Pareto-dominates pad_threshold=0.5/top_k=1/T=1.0.
proposals.attacks: val!=MUSTER (checkpoint selection) + Mozart over-production (pad-threshold up) + Scriabin miss-rate (pad-threshold down) — directly the calibration residuals
proposals.how_to_run: 1) For each on-disk ssl_classical_clean epoch ckpt: robust_eval.sh ckpt out.txt <all 14 pieces>; pick argmin mean MUSTER. 2) Knob sweep on the winner: scripts/infer_with_ckpt.py per piece across --pad-threshold {0.3,0.4,0.5,0.6,0.7} x --top-k {1,3} x --temperature {0.8,1.0}, score each with eval_tuplet.py. 3) Report the best GLOBAL setting and the per-piece envelope. No training. If only 2 epochs survive on disk, re-run the from-scratch ssl_classical_clean once with save_top_k=-1 to retain every epoch (one ~8-12h run, but otherwise zero).
proposals.cost: NONE if epochs are on disk (pure inference, ~few GPU-hours of eval); at most one ~8-12 GPU-hour re-run to retain all epochs
proposals.grounding: Directly grounded in OUR observed phenomena: ssl_bigc proved val and MUSTER diverge via over-production; the memory explicitly hypothesizes 'the right ckpt may be an EARLIER epoch with lower over-production' — this proposal just TESTS that. pad_threshold is the literal note-presence decision boundary (sigmoid at model.py:135), so it is the exact knob that trades miss-rate against over-production. This is the cheapest, most certain lever and also produces the per-piece sensitivity map that informs every other proposal.
proposals.expected_outcome: High chance of a small but real MUSTER mean improvement from better checkpoint selection, and a quantified pad-threshold trade-off curve showing whether Scriabin miss-rate and Mozart over-production can be jointly improved by decoding alone (likely a partial trade, not a free lunch — but we'd KNOW). Success = lower all-14 mean than the val-selected ep13 at fixed compute. This also de-risks proposals 1-2 by telling us the calibration baseline.

proposals.title: Measure all 14 ASAP test pieces to establish true standing and locate the real residual (zero training, prerequisite for everything)
proposals.mechanism: We have only evaluated 4-6 of 14 test pieces, so we do NOT actually know our full standing vs the released SOTA — Scriabin may not even be the worst residual. Run BOTH ssl_classical_clean AND the released ckpt through robust_eval.sh on ALL 14 ASAP test pieces (same harness, leak-clean, per-piece MUSTER + per-stream breakdown), producing the complete head-to-head table. This converts an unmeasured uncertainty into a ranked residual list that retargets all data effort.
proposals.attacks: unmeasured pieces (8-10 of 14 unknown) — and by extension re-validates which residual (Scriabin vs something else) actually deserves the data budget
proposals.how_to_run: 1) paths=collect_paths('test') -> the 14 pieces. 2) robust_eval.sh checkpoints/ssl_classical_clean/...ep13.ckpt ours_all14.txt <14 pieces>. 3) robust_eval.sh <released ckpt> released_all14.txt <14 pieces>. 4) Some pieces TIME OUT in music21/MUSTER (Ravel/Prokofiev seen before) — raise the timeout to 600s and/or use --limit-per 1 (shortest performance) which the harness already supports. 5) Tabulate per-piece MeanER + PitchER/MissRate/TimingER deltas; rank residuals.
proposals.cost: NONE (pure inference/eval) — ~a few GPU-hours plus handling the known music21 timeouts
proposals.grounding: Explicitly flagged in the task: 'We have only evaluated 4-6 of the 14 ASAP test pieces -> we don't actually know our full standing.' The harness (robust_eval.sh + eval_tuplet.py + collect_paths('test')) and both ckpts already exist; this is execution, not invention. Cheap, certain, and strictly necessary to avoid over-fitting effort to Scriabin if a different piece is the larger gap.
proposals.expected_outcome: A complete, leak-clean 14-piece head-to-head vs released SOTA: our true mean standing, and the ranked residual list. Concretely answers whether Scriabin (12.3 vs 10.37) is the worst gap or whether an unmeasured piece is larger. This is the measurement that makes proposals 1-3 targetable; without it, any data intervention is partially blind.

proposals.title: Confidence/difficulty-stratified pseudo-label curriculum: harvest MAESTRO by difficulty band to specifically attack the dense-passage miss-rate without amplifying error
proposals.mechanism: A refinement of proposal 1 that turns pseudo-labelling into ACTIVE selection targeting the residual. Stratify the MAESTRO pseudo-pairs by an input-side difficulty proxy computed from the performance MIDI ALONE (no model needed): local note density (notes/sec in sliding windows) and polyphony. Then build the pseudo-pair pool with a CURRICULUM: keep ALL high-confidence easy/medium pairs (broadens the 822 ceiling safely), but for HIGH-density windows keep a pair only under a STRICTER confidence gate (mean note-presence prob > 0.95 AND note-count ratio within 8%). This deliberately mines the few dense passages the model already gets right and excludes the many it drops — giving the retrain a small but CLEAN dense-passage paired signal, which is exactly what's missing (the model drops ~14% of notes on dense fast passages, so naive pseudo-labelling there would amplify the miss-rate error).
proposals.attacks: Scriabin miss-rate on dense fast passages (the explicit residual) + 822 ceiling (broadens paired data) while structurally guarding against error amplification on the hard tail
proposals.how_to_run: 1) scripts/difficulty_stratify.py: compute notes/sec + polyphony histograms per MAESTRO MIDI from pretty_midi, bucket windows into low/med/high density. 2) Reuse proposal-1 pseudo-labelling + sidecar probs. 3) filter_pseudo.py applies density-conditioned gates (lenient on low/med, strict on high). 4) Optionally oversample the surviving high-density pairs 2-3x in the paired-branch sampler so they aren't drowned out. 5) train.py --dataset-type ssl exposure-matched; robust_eval.sh all 14 pieces, focus on Scriabin MissRate component vs ssl_classical_clean.
proposals.cost: Incremental over proposal 1: ~2-3 extra GPU-hours (stratification is CPU on the 252-core box; one shared training run if combined with proposal 1) = effectively bundled into proposal 1's ~15-20 GPU-hours
proposals.grounding: Difficulty/active selection for self-training is established (curriculum pseudo-labelling, uncertainty-aware selection). Grounded in OUR specific diagnosis: the failure is miss-rate on DENSE passages, not tuplet tokens — so a dense-but-clean paired signal is the theoretically-correct missing ingredient. Speculative because clean high-density MAESTRO passages where the model is BOTH confident AND note-count-correct may be SCARCE (if the model drops 14% of notes there, few windows pass the 8% gate), so the harvested dense set could be too small to move Scriabin. But it cannot HURT (gates prevent amplification) and the easy/medium pairs still help broad accuracy.
proposals.expected_outcome: Best case: a measurable Scriabin (and other dense-piece) MissRate reduction toward the released 10.37, with no Mozart over-production. Realistic case: broad-accuracy gain from easy/medium pairs (like proposal 1) plus a tiny-or-null dense-tail gain if clean dense pairs are too rare. We'd know by tracking the MissRate stream specifically on Scriabin/Ravel and the count of surviving high-density pairs (if <100, the dense lever is starved -> report as field-wide-hard confirmation rather than a failure).

lens: corpus-balance: fix OVER-PRODUCTION by corpus COMPOSITION (note-density / tuplet-rate distribution matching, curriculum, per-sample exposure weighting), not by adding more data

proposals.title: Density-matched unpaired corpus: reshape (not grow) the SSL pool to the ASAP-test note-density histogram
proposals.mechanism: Compute per-score note-density d = n_notes / max(n_measures,1) directly from each unpaired manifest's existing columns (data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv has n_notes, n_measures; the curated-scrape rows can be measure-counted the same way). Compute the SAME d for the held-out ASAP TRAIN pieces (we have their tokenized pairs) to get a target density histogram in, say, 8 bins. Then RESAMPLE the unpaired manifest (subsample over-represented bins, up-sample under-represented bins by row duplication) so the unpaired d-histogram MATCHES the ASAP target. Net corpus size stays ~24-29k (no growth), only the SHAPE changes: down-weight the ultra-dense etude tail (d>~25, the tuplet-dense Chopin/Scriabin scores that biased ssl_bigc) and up-weight moderate pieces (d~6-16). Write the reshaped manifest, train ssl exactly as ssl_classical_clean (same recipe/epochs), pointing --manifest at it.
proposals.attacks: Mozart over-production (the curated corpus is complexity-skewed toward tuplet-dense etudes -> prior over-emits tuplets on simple pieces) AND the Scriabin tail simultaneously: matching the test density shape keeps enough dense exposure for Scriabin while removing the etude over-representation that wrecked Mozart in ssl_bigc.
proposals.grounding: ssl_bigc is the existing proof: adding 5.4k curated scores (complexity-skewed, etudes are tuplet-dense) drove Mozart from 36 to 166 emitted tuplets and regressed Ravel 20.51->24.74 while best-val improved -> the failure is DISTRIBUTION SHAPE, not size. Density d is already in the manifest (verified locally: p10=6, p50=10, p90=17, max=191 notes/measure), so the over-dense tail is concretely identifiable and trimmable. The released model matched its corpus to real repertoire; our pop->classical genre switch already proved corpus COMPOSITION moves the tail (recovered Scriabin 15.15->12.3).
proposals.how_to_run: On the box: (1) python script reads data/pairs_classical_clean_manifest.csv, adds d column, bins it; reads ASAP train tokenized pairs to build target histogram; emits data/pairs_density_matched_manifest.csv (duplicating/subsampling rows to match). (2) cd MIDI2ScoreTransformer/midi2scoretransformer && python train.py fit --stage ssl_densitymatch --dataset-type ssl --manifest data/pairs_density_matched_manifest.csv --init none --lr 3e-4 --max-epochs 15 --warmup-steps 800 --real-fraction 0.5 --batch-size 16 --out-dir checkpoints/ssl_densitymatch (mirror run_ssl_classical_clean.sh). (3) Evaluate every saved epoch with scripts/robust_eval.sh on Mozart+Scriabin+Ravel, tracking emitted-tuplet count per piece.
proposals.cost: ~1 GPU-hour for the reshape+caching is already done (reuses existing caches); one ssl run ~= ssl_classical_clean cost, ~6-10 A100-hours for 15 epochs.
proposals.expected_outcome: Scriabin <= ssl_classical_clean's 12.3 (ideally toward 11.8 that ssl_bigc reached) WITHOUT Mozart over-producing (emitted tuplets back to ~36 not 166, MeanER stays ~5.76) and Ravel staying ~20.5. We KNOW it worked if Scriabin drops while Mozart's emitted-tuplet count and MeanER hold. If Mozart over-produces again, the density axis alone is insufficient and we escalate to tuplet-rate matching (proposal 2).
proposals.risk: Density (notes/measure) is a proxy for tuplet-rate; a score can be dense without tuplets (fast scalar runs) or have tuplets while moderate-density. If the correlation is weak, matching density won't move tuplet behavior. Mitigated by proposal 2 which matches the tuplet token rate directly.

proposals.title: Tuplet-rate balancing: bucket the unpaired corpus by actual tuplet token fraction and hold it to the test rate
proposals.mechanism: The over-production symptom is literally about TUPLETS (166 vs 36 emitted), so balance on the tuplet rate itself rather than a density proxy. For every cached unpaired score (data/cache_pdmx/*.pkl already holds the tokenized output_stream), compute tuplet_rate = fraction of notes whose bucketed duration/offset token is a non-power-of-two (tuplet) value -- this is a pure read of the cached MXL output streams, no re-parsing. Build the SAME statistic for the ASAP TRAIN pieces. Then compose the unpaired manifest so its corpus-level tuplet_rate equals the ASAP-train rate (subsample the high-tuplet etude scores, keep the moderate ones). This directly controls the prior over the tuplet vocabulary that is over-firing.
proposals.attacks: Mozart over-production directly (166->~36 emitted tuplets is a tuplet-prior calibration failure) while preserving SOME high-tuplet scores so Scriabin's tail has exposure. Also gives us the first quantitative readout of how skewed the curated corpus's tuplet rate actually is.
proposals.grounding: The residual is explicitly characterized as a tuplet-token over-emission problem (Mozart 166 vs 36), and the cause is named: 'the curated corpus is complexity-skewed (etudes are tuplet-dense), so more of it biases the notation prior toward over-emitting tuplets.' The output streams are already tokenized and cached (pdmx_dataset._load_pair loads (input_stream, output_stream) from cache_pdmx/*.pkl), and tuplet-ness is exactly a function of the bucketed duration/offset token, so tuplet_rate is computable offline with zero GPU. This is the most direct possible attack on the named mechanism.
proposals.how_to_run: On the box: (1) script iterates the manifest, torch.load each cache pkl, reads output_stream['duration'] / ['offset'] bucket indices, classifies tuplet vs non-tuplet per the tokenizer's bucket table (config.FEATURES), writes tuplet_rate per id. (2) Build target rate from ASAP train caches the same way. (3) Subsample to match -> data/pairs_tupletbalanced_manifest.csv. (4) Train identically to ssl_classical_clean with --manifest pointing at it; eval every epoch on Mozart/Scriabin/Ravel tracking emitted-tuplet counts.
proposals.cost: Offline tuplet-rate pass: ~1-2 CPU-hours on the 252-core box (embarrassingly parallel over caches), no GPU. One ssl train run ~6-10 A100-hours.
proposals.expected_outcome: Mozart emitted tuplets ~= ground-truth 36 (vs ssl_bigc's 166) with MeanER ~5.76, AND we can plot Scriabin MeanER vs corpus tuplet_rate to find the rate that helps Scriabin most before Mozart breaks -- turning over-production into a tunable dial. Success = a corpus tuplet_rate where Scriabin <12.3 and Mozart tuplets <=~50.
proposals.risk: Even with correct tuplet exposure, the model may still emit ~0 Scriabin tuplets (the field-wide-hard tail) -- balancing fixes Mozart over-production but may not by itself crack Scriabin. That's acceptable: removing over-production is required before any tail lever can be measured cleanly.

proposals.title: Simple->dense curriculum on the unpaired stream (sort by density, anneal the cap)
proposals.mechanism: Keep the full classical_clean corpus but change ORDER of exposure: train in phases where the unpaired pool's max-density cap rises over epochs. Phase 1 (epochs 1-5): only moderate scores d<=12 (learn clean, non-over-producing notation). Phase 2 (epochs 6-10): add d up to ~20. Phase 3 (epochs 11-15): full corpus incl. the dense etude tail. Implement by swapping the --manifest between phases (resume from the prior phase's checkpoint), or add an epoch-indexed density cap inside UnpairedScoreDataset that filters self.metadata by the d column. Real ASAP stays 50/50 throughout (val unchanged).
proposals.attacks: Mozart over-production + val!=MUSTER + the ssl_bigc failure mode. By learning the simple notation prior FIRST and only late-introducing dense etudes, the model anchors on correct simple-piece behavior (no early tuplet over-bias) and adds dense capability without overwriting it -- the opposite of dumping all complexity in from step 0.
proposals.grounding: We already observed exposure ORDER/amount matters enormously: ssl_unpaired (each score ~0.5x) vs ssl_v2 (exposure-matched ~12x) flipped Scriabin from 12.68 to 15.15; genre order/composition recovered it. Curriculum is the natural next axis once size is exhausted. The density column needed to define the curriculum is already in the manifest (verified). This reuses the existing ssl path and ModelCheckpoint resume -- no new training machinery.
proposals.how_to_run: On the box: precompute d per row in classical_clean manifest; write 3 nested manifests (d<=12, d<=20, full). Run train.py fit --dataset-type ssl --manifest <phase1> --max-epochs 5 ...; then --init-ckpt <phase1 best> --manifest <phase2> --max-epochs 5; then phase3. Eval every epoch boundary on Mozart/Scriabin/Ravel. (Lower-effort variant: add a --density-cap-schedule flag read by UnpairedScoreDataset to do it in one run.)
proposals.cost: Same total step budget as one ssl_classical_clean run (~6-10 A100-hours); curriculum just reorders the same data. ~0 extra cost.
proposals.expected_outcome: An epoch trajectory where Mozart MeanER stays flat ~5.76 (no over-production spike) while Scriabin descends as dense data is introduced late -- a Pareto improvement over ssl_bigc's 'best val but over-produces'. Known good if at the final phase Scriabin <12.3 while Mozart tuplets stay near 36.
proposals.risk: Late introduction of dense etudes could re-trigger over-production in the final phase (same as ssl_bigc), just delayed. Mitigated by checkpoint-selecting the phase-2 epoch if phase-3 over-produces -- and by combining with proposal 2's tuplet-rate cap on the dense phase.

proposals.title: Per-sample inverse-density loss/exposure weighting in the WeightedRandomSampler (1-line lever)
proposals.mechanism: make_ssl_loaders already builds a WeightedRandomSampler with uniform weights inside the unpaired branch ([(1-rf)/n_unp]*n_unp). Replace those uniform unpaired weights with weights INVERSELY proportional to a density/tuplet score so ultra-dense etudes are sampled LESS and moderate scores MORE, smoothly de-biasing the prior without hard cuts. Weight_i = (1-rf) * w_i / sum(w_i) where w_i = 1/(1 + d_i/d_ref) (gentle downweight of the dense tail). real_fraction stays 0.5; real weights unchanged. This is a soft, continuous version of proposals 1-2, fully inside the existing sampler.
proposals.attacks: Mozart over-production via a continuous knob (no manifest surgery), and lets us sweep the down-weight strength to map the over-production vs Scriabin trade-off cheaply.
proposals.grounding: The sampler and per-row weight vector already exist in make_ssl_loaders (train.py:577-580); we have the per-row density from the manifest. Changing only the weight vector is the minimal, lowest-risk edit and reuses the entire proven ssl_classical_clean recipe. WeightedRandomSampler with replacement=True is already how real/unpaired balance is enforced, so per-sample weighting is the native mechanism, not a bolt-on.
proposals.how_to_run: Edit make_ssl_loaders to accept a density-weight exponent gamma; load d from manifest column; set unpaired weights ~ 1/(1+d/d_ref)**gamma normalized to (1-rf). Add --density-gamma to the fit CLI. Run a small sweep gamma in {0, 0.5, 1.0} (gamma=0 reproduces ssl_classical_clean as a control). Eval each on Mozart/Scriabin/Ravel.
proposals.cost: Code: ~20 lines. Each gamma run ~6-10 A100-hours; a 3-point sweep ~20-30 A100-hours (or 1 run if compute-constrained, gamma=0.5).
proposals.expected_outcome: Monotone curve: as gamma rises, Mozart tuplet over-production falls; we pick the gamma where Scriabin is best subject to Mozart MeanER <= 5.9. Control (gamma=0) must reproduce 5.76/12.3/20.5 to validate the harness.
proposals.risk: Soft down-weighting may be too gentle to move Scriabin (the dense scores still appear, just less often). If so, escalate to the hard density-matched subset (proposal 1). Low risk because gamma=0 is a guaranteed-safe fallback that reproduces current SOTA-parity.

proposals.title: Over-production-aware checkpoint selection: evaluate all 14 test pieces + add a calibration metric, since val!=MUSTER
proposals.mechanism: We currently checkpoint on val/total, but ssl_bigc proved val is anti-correlated with MUSTER when over-production sets in (best val 0.4996, worse MUSTER). Replace val-based selection with a cheap held-out CALIBRATION probe: for each saved epoch, run inference on a small fixed set of SIMPLE held-out scores (low-density, few/no tuplets) and a few DENSE ones, and measure emitted-tuplet-count error (|emitted - ground_truth|) -- an over-production detector. Select the epoch that minimizes Scriabin-tail error subject to simple-piece tuplet-count error below a threshold. Simultaneously evaluate ALL 14 ASAP test pieces (we've only scored 4-6) so we actually know our standing.
proposals.attacks: val!=MUSTER (the named checkpoint-selection bug) AND the unmeasured-pieces gap (we've evaluated only 4-6/14). It also operationalizes 'the right ckpt may be an EARLIER epoch with lower over-production' directly.
proposals.grounding: Stated facts: 'ssl_bigc had the BEST val but WORSE MUSTER (it over-produced) -> val-based checkpoint selection is likely SUBOPTIMAL; the right ckpt may be an EARLIER epoch with lower over-production' and 'We have only evaluated 4-6 of the 14 ASAP test pieces -> we don't actually know our full standing.' The eval harness (robust_eval.sh, per-piece OS-timeout) already exists; this just runs it on all saved epochs and all pieces and adds a tuplet-count tally to the existing inference output.
proposals.how_to_run: On the box: for the existing ssl_classical_clean AND ssl_bigc checkpoint directories, loop saved epochs through scripts/infer_with_ckpt.py on all 14 ASAP test pieces (handle the Prokofiev makeNotation timeout via the harness's OS-timeout), log MeanER + emitted-vs-GT tuplet counts per piece. Pick the epoch minimizing mean MeanER over the 14 (or the calibration-constrained objective). No retraining required.
proposals.cost: Zero GPU training; pure inference + eval. ~2-4 GPU-hours of inference across epochs x 14 pieces (most pieces are fast; Prokofiev may time out and be excluded as already noted).
proposals.expected_outcome: Either (a) an earlier ssl_classical_clean/ssl_bigc epoch beats the val-selected one on full-14 mean MeanER (a free win from re-selection), and/or (b) the first complete 14-piece standing vs released SOTA -- which may reveal we already beat SOTA on more pieces than the 2 known, or expose new residuals to target. Known by the per-piece table.
proposals.risk: May confirm val-selection was already near-optimal (no free epoch), in which case the payoff is purely the full-14 measurement -- still valuable because we currently fly blind on 8-10 pieces. Prokofiev timeout persists (pre-existing, not introduced here).

proposals.title: Expose the continuous pad-sigmoid in generate() and sweep the REAL note-keep threshold (the current --pad-threshold is a no-op)
proposals.mechanism: In MIDI2ScoreTransformer/midi2scoretransformer/models/model.py, generate() stores the pad stream as the HARD argmax of [1-sigmoid, sigmoid] at line 142 (`next_token = probs.argmax(-1, keepdim=True)`), then floats it at line 160. So y['pad'] is binary {0.0,1.0} decided at an implicit sigmoid=0.5. detokenize_mxl then does `pad > pad_threshold` on this binary stream, which means ANY --pad-threshold in (0,1) is identical and >=1.0 drops everything: --pad-threshold is currently a NO-OP for the keep/drop decision. The note-keep gate is hard-locked at 0.5 inside the AR loop. FIX (3 lines, no retrain): keep argmax for the AR feedback token (so the autoregressive state is unchanged and BYTE-IDENTICAL to released behavior at 0.5), but additionally store the continuous sigmoid probability into a parallel tensor y['pad_prob'] = F.sigmoid(logits) and return it; in detokenize_mxl threshold on the continuous pad_prob instead of the binary pad. Then the existing --pad-threshold knob becomes live: LOWERING it below 0.5 rescues the ~14% dropped notes on dense Scriabin passages where the keep-probability is 0.35-0.5.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin miss-rate (the ~14% dropped notes on dense fast passages — the dominant residual). Does NOT touch tuplet tokens, which are unrepresentable on the 1/24 duration grid anyway.
proposals.how_to_run: On box A100: edit model.py generate() to emit y['pad_prob'] (continuous sigmoid) alongside the binary y['pad']; edit detokenize_mxl to use pad_prob for the mask when present (fall back to pad otherwise — preserves released A/B). Then sweep with scripts/infer_with_ckpt.py + benchmark/eval_tuplet.py via robust_eval.sh on the 4-6 eval pieces: `for T in 0.50 0.45 0.40 0.35 0.30 0.25; do ./venv311/bin/python benchmark/eval_tuplet.py --ckpt ssl_classical_clean --pieces Scriabin Ravel Mozart Prokofiev Liszt --pad-threshold $T --out benchmark/padsweep_$T.json; done`. Per piece, log MeanER AND emitted-note-count vs ground-truth count to watch over-production. Verify --pad-threshold=0.5 reproduces the current 12.3 Scriabin / 5.76 Mozart numbers exactly (sanity that the continuous threshold collapses to the old binary behavior at 0.5).
proposals.cost: ~0 GPU-hours of training; ~10-20 min/piece x 6 thresholds x 5 pieces on one A100 (pure inference). Call it 3-6 GPU-hours total, fully overnight-able tonight on ssl_classical_clean.
proposals.grounding: Directly read from source: model.py line 142 argmax + line 160 float = binary pad stream; detokenize_mxl line 463 `pad > pad_threshold`. The miss-rate is explicitly the stated residual and is a pure keep/drop calibration symptom. The docstring at tokenizer.py:455 even claims 'Lowering to ~0.3-0.4 rescues notes' — but that effect is currently unreachable because the upstream stream is binary. This is the rare case where a documented knob is silently dead.
proposals.expected_outcome: Scriabin MeanER drops toward the released 10.37 as more true notes are rescued; we know it worked if Scriabin emitted-note-count rises toward its GT count and MeanER falls, while Mozart note-count stays ~its GT (Mozart's kept notes already have high pad-prob, so a lower threshold should barely change it — this is the key test that it fixes miss-rate WITHOUT re-introducing Mozart over-production). If Mozart over-produces at low threshold, that bounds the usable threshold. Target: Scriabin < 12.3 by >=0.5 MeanER at a threshold (e.g. 0.35) where Mozart stays within +/-0.1 of 5.76.
proposals.risk: Low. Worst case the rescued notes are wrong-pitch/timing and MeanER doesn't improve, but it cannot regress the released-config A/B (we preserve 0.5 == binary behavior). Slight chance the continuous sigmoid is poorly calibrated and the sweep is flat — but that itself is a publishable finding about why the tail is hard.

proposals.title: Per-head decoding: greedy pad/duration, low-temperature pitch — decouple the globally-shared top_k/temperature
proposals.mechanism: infer() and generate() apply a SINGLE top_k and temperature to ALL 13 output heads uniformly (model.py lines 101,128-130). This is crude: the pad (keep), duration, and offset heads want pure greedy/argmax (they're structural and over-sampling them causes ragged rhythm + spurious notes = over-production), while the pitch head on dense polyphony benefits from a slightly higher top_k to recover notes the argmax-pitch collapses onto a neighbor. Mechanism: thread a per-head dict of (top_k, temperature) through infer->generate, replacing the scalar branch at lines 101/128. Concretely: pad/offset/duration/downbeat = greedy (top_k=1, T=1.0, i.e. current released behavior, untouched); pitch = top_k=3, T~1.05 (mild). Everything else greedy. This leaves the rhythm/structure spine identical to the released model (so Mozart structure is untouched) and only loosens the pitch head where the miss/pitch residual lives.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin pitch-stream error (the second-largest residual after miss-rate) without perturbing the rhythm spine that drives Mozart over-production. Surgically separates 'recover hard pitches' from 'emit more notes'.
proposals.how_to_run: On box: add `head_overrides: dict|None` param to generate()/infer(); in the per-k loop use `tk, temp = head_overrides.get(k, (top_k, temperature))`. Add CLI flags to infer_with_ckpt.py: `--pitch-top-k 3 --pitch-temp 1.05`. Sweep on eval pieces: pitch-top-k in {1,2,3,5} x pitch-temp in {1.0,1.05,1.1}, all other heads greedy. Compare vs the global-top_k baseline (which is the tested released behavior). Use robust_eval.sh wrapper.
proposals.cost: ~0 training. Inference sweep ~4-8 GPU-hours for the grid x 5 pieces on one A100. Overnight-able.
proposals.grounding: Read from model.py: top_k/temperature are scalar and shared across the for-k loop (lines 97-149). Pitch is a 128-way head decoded by multinomial; a top_k=1 pitch on dense chords is exactly where a model 'drops' a note by collapsing two near-simultaneous pitches. Keeping pad/duration greedy preserves the structural calibration that, when loosened by global top_k>1 or T>1, would cause over-production — this is why a global temperature bump is dangerous but a pitch-only bump is not.
proposals.expected_outcome: Scriabin MeanER improves via fewer pitch errors; Mozart MeanER and note-count essentially unchanged (its rhythm spine is byte-identical to released decoding). We know it worked if Scriabin pitch-substream error drops in eval_tuplet's per-stream breakdown while Mozart over-production metric (emitted/GT note ratio) is flat. If even mild pitch sampling raises variance and hurts, fall back to top_k=1 — bounded downside.
proposals.risk: Multinomial sampling on pitch makes outputs stochastic; must fix a seed and ideally average 2-3 samples per piece for a stable MeanER read. Adds plumbing but no architecture change.

proposals.title: Overlap-window voting / self-ensemble using the existing chunked AR loop to stabilize dense-passage notes
proposals.mechanism: infer() already runs the decoder in overlapping chunks (chunk=512, overlap=128, lines 78-95) but currently just DISCARDS the overlap region of each new chunk (line 89 `y_hat[:, overlap:]`), keeping only the first chunk's version of overlapping notes. On dense Scriabin, a note near a chunk boundary is decoded once with little/lots of context and may be dropped. Mechanism: run the SAME piece under 2-3 different chunk phasings (e.g. offsets 0, 256, 384 into the stride) so every note is decoded in >=2 different context windows, then VOTE per input-note: keep the note if it survives the pad gate in a majority of windows, and for kept notes take the majority pitch/duration. Because the AR loop is indexed by encoder input-note position, the alignment of votes across phasings is exact (same note index), making the vote trivial and principled.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin miss-rate, specifically notes dropped due to unlucky chunk-boundary context. Also reduces variance from the stochastic pitch sampling in proposal 2. Mozart is short/simple so phasings agree -> no change (no over-production).
proposals.how_to_run: On box: wrapper around infer() that calls it 3x with different starting offsets (pad the input head by 0/256/384 zero-notes then strip), aligns the per-note streams by absolute input index, and votes. Add `--vote-phasings 3` to infer_with_ckpt.py. Evaluate on the 5-6 pieces with eval_tuplet via robust_eval.sh; compare 1-phasing (current) vs 3-phasing.
proposals.cost: ~0 training. 3x inference cost = ~9-15 GPU-hours for the eval set. Overnight-able; can stack on top of proposal 1's exposed pad-prob (vote on pad-prob, even cleaner).
proposals.grounding: The chunking + overlap machinery already exists and currently throws away the redundant decode (line 89). Notes near boundaries provably get a single, possibly context-starved decode. Voting across phasings is the standard fix and is cheap here because the encoder-input indexing gives exact note correspondence (no DTW needed). Simple pieces produce identical decodes across phasings, so it cannot induce Mozart over-production.
proposals.expected_outcome: Scriabin miss-rate falls (more boundary notes rescued), MeanER improves modestly; Mozart unchanged (identical votes). Know it worked if Scriabin recovered-note-count rises and the per-piece MeanER variance across reruns shrinks. Most powerful when combined with proposal 1 (average the continuous pad-prob across phasings before thresholding).
proposals.risk: If chunk boundaries aren't actually where notes drop (drops are uniform), gain is small. 3x compute. No correctness risk.

proposals.title: Measure the full 14-piece ASAP test split at multiple thresholds to find our true standing and the over-production frontier
proposals.mechanism: We have only evaluated 4-6 of 14 ASAP test pieces, so we DON'T know our real aggregate standing vs released SOTA, and we're tuning the pad/temperature knobs on a tiny, possibly-unrepresentative slice. Mechanism: run the complete 14-piece ASAP test split through robust_eval.sh for BOTH ssl_classical_clean and the RELEASED ckpt, at the default config AND at the 2-3 best pad-thresholds from proposal 1. Produce a per-piece table of (MeanER, emitted/GT note ratio) for both models. This converts 'we think we have parity' into a measured aggregate and, critically, reveals on which pieces a lower pad-threshold helps (dense) vs hurts (simple) — directly mapping the over-production frontier.
proposals.attacks: The 'unmeasured pieces' phenomenon AND val!=MUSTER: gives a defensible aggregate MUSTER number and identifies whether ssl_classical_clean's checkpoint/threshold choice generalizes beyond Scriabin/Mozart/Ravel, or whether the wins are cherry-picked.
proposals.how_to_run: On box: `bash scripts/robust_eval.sh ssl_classical_clean benchmark/full_clean.txt <all 14 piece substrings>` and same for the released ckpt, at pad-thresholds {0.50, best1, best2}. Parse into a per-piece MeanER + note-ratio table. Reuse the exact eval_tuplet/robust_eval path so numbers are comparable to the validated 5.76/20.51.
proposals.cost: ~0 training. ~14 pieces x 3 thresholds x 2 models x ~15 min = ~20-30 GPU-hours, but trivially parallelizable across the 252-core box / multiple A100 streams; realistically one overnight run.
proposals.grounding: Stated directly: only 4-6/14 measured, val!=MUSTER (ssl_bigc had best val but worse MUSTER via over-production), so checkpoint/threshold selection on a 6-piece slice is high-variance. You cannot claim aggregate parity or choose a global pad-threshold without the full split. This is the cheapest way to de-risk every other proposal's threshold choice.
proposals.expected_outcome: A defensible aggregate MUSTER MeanER for ssl_classical_clean vs released across all 14, plus a per-piece note-ratio frontier telling us the single global pad-threshold that minimizes mean MeanER without pushing simple pieces' note-ratio above ~1.05. Know it worked when we have the table and can name the threshold; if the chosen threshold helps Scriabin but several simple pieces over-produce, that quantifies the calibration trade-off precisely.
proposals.risk: Pure measurement — no model risk. Only risk is MUSTER/music21 C-hangs, already mitigated by robust_eval.sh's 220s OS-level timeout. Some pieces may TIMEOUT and need reruns.

proposals.title: Beat-phase-consistent offset decoding: nudge the offset head toward the input MIDI's own onset grid on dense passages (speculative)
proposals.mechanism: The encoder input carries the performance MIDI onsets (onset_stream, tokenizer.py line 108) and the model already has a beat_phase stream. On dense fast passages the offset head (1/24 grid) is where timing error concentrates and where notes get squashed onto the same offset bucket and then dropped as duplicates. Mechanism (inference-only, grammar-style like the existing downbeat/accidental constraints at model.py lines 103-126): for each decode step, compute the input note's relative onset within its beat from the MIDI onset_stream, and ADD a small log-prior to the offset head's logits favoring the few buckets consistent with that input onset phase (a soft Gaussian bump in log-space), rather than letting the offset head freely collapse. This is a soft constraint: it only re-weights, never forces. It exploits that the MIDI timing, however expressive, monotonically tracks the notated offset ordering.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin timing-substream error and the duplicate-offset collapse that contributes to miss-rate on dense passages. Mozart is rhythmically simple so the prior agrees with the free decode -> negligible change, no over-production.
proposals.how_to_run: On box: in generate(), at the k=='offset' branch, fetch the current step's input onset from x['onset'] (available via encoder_hidden_states alignment by step index), compute beat-relative phase, build a log-Gaussian over offset buckets centered there with tunable width sigma and weight lambda, add to logits before top_k. Add `--offset-prior-lambda` / `--offset-prior-sigma` to infer_with_ckpt.py. Sweep lambda in {0,0.5,1,2}, sigma in {1,2,3 buckets} on Scriabin+Mozart+Ravel via robust_eval.sh. lambda=0 must reproduce baseline exactly.
proposals.cost: ~0 training. Inference sweep ~6-10 GPU-hours for the small grid x 3-5 pieces. Overnight-able.
proposals.grounding: The constraint-injection pattern is already proven in this exact code (downbeat forced on offset-decrease line 104, accidental legality lines 107-126) — so the hook and idiom exist. The input onset stream is present and is the strongest available side-information about notated timing. The 1/24 grid limits resolution but offset ORDER is reliable. Marked speculative because prior beat-conditioning FINE-TUNES were neutral-to-destructive (per memory) — but that was at TRAINING time with a learned token; this is a SOFT INFERENCE prior with lambda=0 guaranteed safe, a different and cheaper intervention that can't drift MUSTER the way fine-tuning did.
proposals.expected_outcome: On Scriabin, fewer offset-bucket collisions -> fewer dropped duplicate notes and lower timing-substream error -> MeanER down. Mozart unchanged (prior agrees). Know it worked if Scriabin timing substream + miss-rate both fall while Mozart is flat; if lambda>0 ever hurts a simple piece, cap lambda. Given the failed beat fine-tune, treat a null result as likely (~40%) but the lambda=0 guarantee makes it free to try.
proposals.risk: Speculative: the failed beat-conditioning experiment suggests beat side-info may not help this model. But this is inference-only and lambda=0-safe, so downside is just wasted GPU-hours, not a regression. Plumbing the per-step input-onset alignment is the main effort.

lens: literature-contrarian (2023-2026 piano AMT / MIDI-to-score / symbolic-music / rhythm-quantization literature, mapped onto the ssl_classical_clean asset stack)

proposals.title: Self-distilled pad-head recalibration: tune the per-note keep-gate against MUSTER, not val-loss
proposals.mechanism: The model is a 1-output-token-per-input-note AR decoder with a single per-note binary 'pad' head (sigmoid, default threshold 0.5 in detokenize_mxl). Notes are DROPPED only when sigmoid(pad)<threshold — so the entire 14-22% miss-rate on dense passages, AND the Mozart over-production, are both literally one scalar gate (plus its calibration). Three nested, escalating interventions, all on ssl_classical_clean with NO retrain: (1) Sweep --pad-threshold per-piece-density (e.g. 0.30-0.50) and globally; dense Scriabin/Ravel want a LOWER threshold (rescue dropped notes), simple Mozart wants a HIGHER one (kill over-emission). (2) Calibrate the pad logit with a single temperature/bias fit by EM-style self-distillation: run the model on the ~200hr unaligned MAESTRO, collect predicted note-COUNT per chunk, and fit one affine transform (a,b) on the pad logit so predicted-kept-count matches the histogram of the input MIDI note-count (input note count is an upper bound and a strong prior for how many score notes exist) — this is the 'Count The Notes / CountEM' histogram-supervision idea (arXiv:2511.14250, ISMIR'25) reduced to its cheapest form: counts, not alignment. (3) If a single global (a,b) helps, fold it into a 1-epoch fine-tune that adds a count-matching auxiliary loss on the pad stream over MAESTRO.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin/dense miss-rate (rescue dropped notes via lower gate) AND Mozart over-production (raise gate / count-match) AND val!=MUSTER (selects the gate by MUSTER directly, bypassing the misleading val number)
proposals.how_to_run: Stage 1 (free, hours): on the box, scripts/robust_eval.sh over a --pad-threshold grid via infer_with_ckpt.py (already exposes --pad-threshold) on all 14 ASAP test pieces; pick per-density-bin optimum. eval_tuplet.py already reports tuplet_ratio + MeanER so the over-production signal is read directly. Stage 2 (free): add a ~30-line script that runs forward_dec over MAESTRO, dumps pad logits + input note counts, fits (a,b) by least-squares to match kept-count to a target fraction of input-count, patch detokenize_mxl to apply a*logit+b. Stage 3 (~3-6 GPU-hr): 1-epoch train.py fine-tune of ssl_classical_clean with an added BCE-to-count auxiliary on pad.
proposals.cost: Stage 1-2: none (CPU/box inference, minutes-hours). Stage 3: ~3-6 GPU-hours on the A100.
proposals.grounding: Verified in code: detokenize_mxl gates every note on sigmoid(pad)>pad_threshold and the docstring itself says 'Lowering to ~0.3-0.4 rescues notes'; generate() treats pad uniquely (sigmoid, no argmax). Decomposed findings quantify the failure as Miss 17-22%/Extra 13-15% in dense passages — i.e. a gate-calibration symptom, not a capacity wall. The memory explicitly flags val!=MUSTER and that the right ckpt is the one with less over-production. CountEM (2511.14250) shows note-count histograms alone are sufficient supervision for AMT. This is the single cheapest lever that touches BOTH residuals at once and has never been swept per-piece.
proposals.expected_outcome: Per-piece pad-threshold sweep should move Scriabin below 12.3 (rescuing some of the dropped ~14%) while a higher threshold drops Mozart tuplet over-emission back toward 36; success = MeanER drops on Scriabin/Ravel with Mozart NOT regressing past 5.86, measured directly by robust_eval.sh across all 14 pieces. A clean win even of 0.5-1.0 MeanER on the dense tail with no Mozart cost would be decisive and essentially free.
proposals.risk: The pad head may be well-calibrated already on simple pieces (threshold sweep flat there); count-matching could trade pitch errors for recall (rescued notes may be wrong-pitch). Mitigated because eval is direct-on-MUSTER, so any regression is visible immediately and reversible (inference-only).

proposals.title: Multi-sample self-consistency decode with note-level majority voting (free recall on dense passages)
proposals.mechanism: Decoding is currently strictly greedy (top_k=1, temperature=1, single pass — confirmed in utils.infer/model.generate). For dense chunks the greedy path commits early to dropping notes (pad gate) and to wrong pitch/timing under exposure bias. Run N=5-9 stochastic decodes per piece (temperature ~0.7-1.0, top_k>1 — both already CLI-exposed in infer_with_ckpt.py), then AGGREGATE at the note level rather than picking one sequence: because each output note corresponds to a fixed encoder input note (the architecture is a per-input-note translator), the N samples are already aligned by input-note index. For each input-note slot, majority-vote the pad keep/drop and the discrete pitch/duration/voice tokens across the N samples. This is self-consistency (ASR/LLM) specialized to a slot-aligned music decoder, and is far cheaper than it sounds because samples share the encoder pass and KV structure.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin/dense miss-rate (a note dropped in greedy but kept in the majority of samples gets rescued; a spurious Mozart over-emission kept in greedy but dropped by the majority gets removed) — hits BOTH residuals; also lowers variance on unmeasured pieces
proposals.how_to_run: Free, inference-only on the box. Wrap infer_with_ckpt.py in a loop over N seeds with --temperature 0.8 --top-k 4, collect the N y_hat dicts (already slot-aligned by construction since chunking is by note index with fixed 512/128 overlap), add a ~40-line aggregator that majority-votes each stream per slot before detokenize_mxl, then score with robust_eval.sh on all 14 ASAP pieces. A/B vs the greedy ssl_classical_clean number.
proposals.cost: none beyond N× inference (N=5-9; minutes per piece on A100). No training.
proposals.grounding: Confirmed greedy single-pass decoding in utils.infer + model.generate (top_k=1 default, one multinomial draw). Self-consistency/voting reliably beats greedy for structured AR decoding (the calibration/decoding literature in our search: 2509.26376 token-entropy AR scaling; ASR deliberation 2204.07556). The KEY enabling fact is architectural and specific to us: this model is a slot-aligned input-note→output-note translator (one token per input note), so the N samples are trivially aligned and votable WITHOUT sequence alignment — unlike free-form generation where voting is hard. The pad gate being the miss mechanism means voting directly de-noises the keep/drop decision that causes the 14% drop.
proposals.expected_outcome: Majority-vote recall should recover a fraction of the ~14% dropped dense notes and suppress isolated over-emissions; expect Scriabin and Ravel MeanER to drop modestly (0.3-1.0) with Mozart stable or improved. Success criterion: voted output beats greedy on the dense tail across pieces with no simple-piece regression. If entropy-weighted voting (keep the higher-confidence pad decision) beats plain majority, that's a second free gain.
proposals.risk: If the model is near-deterministic at low temperature, samples collapse and voting is a no-op; needs temperature high enough for diversity without degrading pitch accuracy (sweepable for free). Slot alignment across chunk boundaries needs care at the 128-note overlap seams.

proposals.title: DPO/KTO preference fine-tune against a rule-based over-production / miss penalty (no new aligned data)
proposals.mechanism: The binding ceiling is 822 aligned pairs; the residual is a CALIBRATION distribution mismatch (over-produces tuplets on Mozart, drops notes on Scriabin), not a missing-supervision problem. Direct Preference Optimization sidesteps the 822 ceiling because it needs NO new gold scores — only PREFERENCE pairs the model generates itself, scored by cheap rules. Recipe (exactly the Meng et al. ICASSP'26 'rule-based constraints → DPO then KTO' framework, arXiv:2604.18489): (1) sample 2-4 decodes per training MIDI from ssl_classical_clean; (2) score each with a rule reward = -|n_pred_tuplets - n_expected| (over-production penalty, where n_expected is estimated from input timing regularity) - missrate_penalty(dropped notes vs input count) - notation_wellformedness_violations; (3) form (preferred, dispreferred) pairs and run DPO to push the model AWAY from over-emitting and dropping; (4) KTO on unpaired bad samples for the cases with no clean positive. This teaches the calibration the val-loss can't, using the model's own outputs.
proposals.attacks: Mozart over-production (directly penalized) AND Scriabin/dense miss-rate (miss penalty) AND val!=MUSTER (the preference reward is a MUSTER-shaped surrogate, optimizing the right objective instead of token cross-entropy)
proposals.how_to_run: ~20-40 GPU-hours on the A100. Reuse the existing classical-clean manifest MIDIs for sampling; write a reward scorer reusing count_tuplets() from eval_tuplet.py + a note-count miss penalty + the notational-rule state machine from Idea 4. Add a DPO loss head to train.py (the decoder already returns per-stream logits; DPO is a log-ratio loss over sampled sequences vs reference = frozen ssl_classical_clean). Evaluate with robust_eval.sh across all 14 ASAP pieces, A/B vs ssl_classical_clean AND the released ckpt.
proposals.cost: ~20-40 GPU-hours (sampling + a few hundred DPO/KTO steps; preference FT converges fast, no full pretrain).
proposals.grounding: arXiv:2604.18489 (ICASSP'26) demonstrates EXACTLY this — rule-based constraints auto-generate a preference set from an SFT model's own outputs, then DPO+KTO removes 'constraint violation' (their term for the over-/under-production analogue) with NO human annotation — which is the precise shape of our problem (over-emission + the 822 ceiling). Our reward primitives already exist in-repo (count_tuplets, note counts, the in-generate grammar rules). DPO is known to fix distributional/calibration miscalibration that MLE can't, and crucially does NOT require more aligned pairs — directly defeating the binding 822 constraint that killed every data-scaling lever.
proposals.expected_outcome: Mozart tuplet count should fall toward the gt 36 (from the over-producing regimes) while dense-tail miss-rate drops, net MeanER improvement on BOTH ends without new data. Success = Scriabin improves AND Mozart holds/improves in the same checkpoint — the exact win that the ssl_bigc data-scaling round could NOT achieve (it traded one for the other). Knowing it worked: a single ckpt that is Pareto-better than ssl_classical_clean across the 14 pieces.
proposals.risk: DPO can over-optimize the proxy reward and degrade real fidelity (reward hacking) — mitigate with KL anchoring to ssl_classical_clean and direct MUSTER eval each few hundred steps. Reward design is the crux; a bad miss-penalty could re-introduce over-production.

proposals.title: Post-hoc notational-grammar corrector: a deterministic state-machine repair pass on the decoded score
proposals.mechanism: Instead of fixing the model, fix its OUTPUT with a cheap, deterministic, ZERO-GPU notation-rule repair stage inserted between detokenize_mxl and postprocess_score. The generate() loop ALREADY contains hard musical constraints applied at decode time (forces a downbeat when offset decreases; masks impossible accidentals per pitch-class) — proving this codebase's own authors found local grammar rules worth enforcing. Extend that idea POST-HOC and globally using the modular state-machine of 'Detecting Notational Errors in Digital Music Scores' (arXiv:2510.02746): rhythm/time inconsistencies within a measure (durations not summing to the time signature → the symptom of dropped/extra notes in dense bars) and context rules (illegal tuplet groupings, voice-crossing, tie/duration contradictions). The repair: where a dense measure's voice has a duration deficit (notes were DROPPED), re-open the pad gate for the lowest-confidence suppressed notes in that measure to fill the deficit; where there's a surplus (OVER-produced), drop the lowest-confidence extra notes. This is a measure-local, MUSTER-aware grammar corrector keyed to exactly the over/under-production signal.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin/dense miss-rate (fill measure-duration deficits from the suppressed-note pool) AND Mozart over-production (trim surplus to satisfy the bar) — both, and improves notational well-formedness that MUSTER rewards
proposals.how_to_run: Free, CPU-only, no model change. Implement the state machine over the music21 stream that detokenize_mxl already returns (reuse the repo's music21 install in venv311). Keep the model's per-note pad LOGITS around (not just the thresholded mask) so the corrector has a confidence-ranked pool to add/remove from. Insert as a function call in infer_with_ckpt.py before postprocess_score, gate it behind a flag, A/B with robust_eval.sh on all 14 ASAP pieces. The ASAP-notational-error work even ships rules tested ON the ASAP scores we evaluate against.
proposals.cost: none (pure CPU post-processing).
proposals.grounding: arXiv:2510.02746 builds an extensible modular state machine for Western-notation rule violations and APPLIES IT TO ASAP — the exact corpus our test split comes from (they found ~40% of ASAP scores have a notational error, so the rules are real and tested there). The repo already enforces local grammar inside generate() (downbeat-on-offset-decrease, accidental masking) — so a post-hoc global version is a natural, low-risk extension the authors implicitly endorsed.
