Because MUSTER penalizes miss/extra and the corrector trims/fills to satisfy bar arithmetic, it pushes directly on the measured quantity. Crucially it has access to the pad confidence ranking (available, just not currently retained), so add/remove decisions are principled.
proposals.expected_outcome: Measure-arithmetic repair should reduce both Miss and Extra in dense bars (the 17-22% / 13-15% figures) → lower MeanER on Scriabin/Ravel and reduced Mozart over-emission. Success = consistent MeanER drop with zero GPU cost; even a partial gain ships immediately since it is inference-only and reversible. Bonus: it may finally produce SOME tuplets correctly where a triplet bar currently fails duration-summation.
proposals.risk: music21 round-trip fragility (the repo already battles MUSTER/music21 hangs on rough output via OS-level timeouts) — the corrector must be defensive and fall back to the unrepaired score on any exception. Aggressive note-adding could insert wrong pitches; keep edits minimal and confidence-gated.

proposals.title: Beat/downbeat-conditioned rhythm refiner as a teacher OR second-stage cleanup (the only published MUSTER-SOTA-beating method we haven't tried)
proposals.mechanism: Wachter/Murgul/Heizmann's beat-based rhythm-quantization line (arXiv:2508.19262 and the extended 2604.22290) is the ONE method that reports BEATING SOTA on the MUSTER metric on ASAP (onset-F1 97.3%, note-value acc 83.3%), and it does so by a fundamentally different decomposition: feed the model a priori BEAT/DOWNBEAT positions and quantize performance onsets to a beat-relative grid before notating. We have never given our model beat conditioning as an INPUT prior (the prior beat-conditioning experiment in memory fine-tuned our model and was neutral-to-destructive, but that conditioned the SAME end-to-end model — NOT a dedicated beat-grid rhythm stage). Two uses on our assets: (A) Teacher/ensemble — run their published beat-quantizer (or reimplement the T5 beat-quantization head) to produce the rhythm/note-value stream, and let our ssl_classical_clean own pitch/voice/spelling; merge per-note (our model already separates these streams). (B) Distillation — use the beat-quantizer's note-value outputs on the 822 ASAP pairs + MAESTRO as soft targets to fine-tune our duration/offset heads, importing its beat-grid inductive bias without changing architecture. Their explicit augmentations — note-deletion and performance-side time-jitter — are precisely simulations of the dense-passage miss + timing noise we fail on.
proposals.attacks: Scriabin timing + the dense-tuplet tail specifically (beat-relative grid is how triplets/quintuplets become representable — recursive integer subdivisions are defined relative to the beat, which we currently lack as an explicit prior) and dense-passage timing error
proposals.how_to_run: Beats for ASAP are FREE (ASAP ships beat+downbeat annotations; the repo's own chunker already does a beat-swap, so beat infra exists). (A) ~none-to-low GPU: obtain beat-quantizer note-values, merge streams, eval with robust_eval.sh. (B) ~10-20 GPU-hr: add their note-deletion + time-jitter augmentation to train.py's renderer and do a short distillation/fine-tune of the duration+offset heads of ssl_classical_clean on beat-gridded targets. A/B vs released ckpt on all 14 pieces, with special attention to Scriabin tuplet_ratio (already reported by eval_tuplet.py).
proposals.cost: Path A: ~none (inference + merge). Path B: ~10-20 GPU-hours.
proposals.grounding: 2508.19262 + 2604.22290 are the only recent papers in our search claiming to EXCEED SOTA on MUSTER on ASAP, via beat-conditioned quantization — a lever orthogonal to our masked-SSL data strategy. Recursive integer subdivision (tuplets) is mathematically defined relative to beats (the survey/our search note: 'bar positions then recursive integer divisions … for tuplets'), so a beat-relative grid is the principled substrate for the unsolved tuplet tail that BOTH our model and the released SOTA emit ~0 of. Their note-deletion/time-jitter augmentations are direct analogues of our dense-passage miss+timing failure. This is contrarian precisely because our prior beat-conditioning test was negative — but that test conditioned the monolithic model rather than installing a dedicated beat-grid rhythm stage, which is what actually wins in their results.
proposals.expected_outcome: If the beat-grid rhythm stage transfers, expect the FIRST non-zero correct tuplet production on Scriabin (current 0/99 for everyone) and a Scriabin MeanER move toward the released 10.37, with timing-stream error dropping on dense pieces. Success = Scriabin tuplet_ratio rises above ~0 with MeanER improving; even partial tuplet recovery would be the first crack in the field-wide-hard tail. Knowing it failed: tuplet_ratio stays ~0 and MeanER flat (cheap to falsify on Path A before spending Path B GPU).
proposals.risk: Highest-risk idea: prior beat-conditioning was neutral-to-destructive, so the beat prior may not help OUR tokenization; merging two models' streams can create notational inconsistencies (mitigated by the grammar corrector in Idea 4). Reimplementing their quantizer is the main effort — do Path A as a cheap go/no-go before committing Path B GPU.
